provenance.source: Recall-gap case (investigated 2026-06-21): findings cursor/qodo/cubic caught that jbot missed on the first run (passes=2, shards=1), plus jbot's own catches. The first run found only the keyboard-nav issue; glm-5.2 caught every miss in isolation, so the gap was context dilution, not model capability.

findings.description: The 'Showing X-Y of N' subtitle derives from/to from the updated `page` while `data` still holds the previous page during refetch (data is never cleared and the `loading && !data` guard keeps stale items rendered), so the header desyncs from the displayed cards on page change. Consensus P2 (cursor + cubic); missed by jbot on the first run.

Remote third-party SVGs from models.dev are written into the same-origin served apps/web/public/logos directory after only a startsWith('<svg') check (no sanitization). A compromised/typo-squatted upstream yields stored XSS / supply-chain risk reachable directly at /logos/*.svg, even though the app consumes them as CSS masks. Consensus P2 (cubic + qodo); missed by jbot on both runs (dismissed as mask-only-safe).

ProviderPicker replaces a native <select> but implements no keyboard navigation (arrow keys, type-to-jump, Enter/Escape), so keyboard-only users lose access the native control had. jbot's own catch on this PR (via the interactions lens) - the recall anchor that must not regress.

The data-fetch effect calls api.jobs.list(...).then(setData).finally(...) with no .catch(): an API failure becomes an unhandled rejection and the user sees a blank/stale page with no error state (the prior api.get() path had a .catch). Bonus bug jbot surfaced after the passes=3/shards=3 retune; not flagged by cursor/qodo/cubic.

Learnings is statically imported at module scope while its route is gated by import.meta.env.DEV, so keeping the dev-only mock out of the production bundle relies on tree-shaking rather than being guaranteed (the Login page, defined in-module, is). Soft P3 - Vite dead-code elimination usually drops it. Caught by qodo and by jbot after the retune.

relativeTime() does not guard invalid/optional input: new Date(bad).getTime() is NaN and the function returns 'NaNd ago' (and new Date(null) maps to the epoch -> '29000d ago'). Soft/low severity; qodo's 'premature rounding' framing is partly overstated (the double-rounding does not cross a unit boundary).
